title: BPE Merge Algorithm
description:
Core BPE tokenization algorithm.

Implement the byte-pair encoding merge loop:
1. Start with input as individual bytes
2. Find the adjacent pair with the lowest rank in the merge table
3. Merge that pair into a single token
4. Repeat until no more merges possible
5. Return list of token byte-strings

Functions:
- merge_loop(tokens, ranks) -> [binary()]
- find_best_pair(tokens, ranks) -> {merged, rank} | nil
- merge_pair(tokens, pair) -> tokens

This is the hot path - should be efficient for small inputs (<1KB).
Consider using :binary operations for performance.
close_reason: BPE merge algorithm implemented and tested.

title: Rank File Loader
description:
Load and parse tiktoken rank files.

Tiktoken rank file format (e.g., cl100k_base.tiktoken):
- One line per token
- Each line: base64(token_bytes) <space> rank_integer
- ~100k lines for cl100k_base, ~200k for o200k_base

Implement:
- load(encoding_name) -> %{binary() => integer()}
- Parse from priv/ranks/ directory
- Cache loaded ranks in ETS or persistent_term for reuse
- Support :cl100k_base and :o200k_base encodings
- Validate file integrity on load

Also handle downloading rank files (or document how to get them).
close_reason: Rank file loader with persistent_term caching. cl100k_base and o200k_base.

title: Public API: encode/decode
description:
Top-level public API for the library.

Functions:
- Tiktokenex.encode(text, encoding \\ :cl100k_base) -> [integer()]
  Returns token IDs.

- Tiktokenex.decode(token_ids, encoding \\ :cl100k_base) -> binary()
  Returns decoded text.

- Tiktokenex.encode_to_chunks(text, encoding \\ :cl100k_base) -> [binary()]
  Returns the actual string chunks (what Quantok needs for its Chunker).
  e.g., encode_to_chunks("Hello world") -> ["Hello", " world"]

- Tiktokenex.count(text, encoding \\ :cl100k_base) -> integer()
  Token count (convenience).

- Tiktokenex.encoding(name) -> %Encoding{}
  Load/cache an encoding for repeated use.

Special tokens:
- Handle <|endoftext|>, <|fim_prefix|>, etc.
- Option to allow or disallow special tokens in input.
close_reason: Public API: encode, decode, encode_to_chunks, count. All tested.

title: Test Suite & Validation
description:
Comprehensive tests validated against tiktoken Python reference.

Tests:
- Unit tests for BPE merge algorithm with known inputs
- Unit tests for rank file loading
- Unit tests for pre-tokenization regex
- Integration tests: encode known strings, compare against tiktoken Python output
- Round-trip tests: encode then decode should return original
- Edge cases: empty string, single char, Unicode, emoji, newlines, special tokens
- Property tests: encode(decode(ids)) == ids for valid ids

Validation approach:
- Generate reference test vectors using Python tiktoken
- Store as test fixtures
- Compare Elixir output against reference

Include a mix task or script to regenerate test vectors from Python.
close_reason: 47 tests passing. Round-trip, reference vectors, edge cases.

title: Regex Pre-tokenization
description:
Implement tiktoken's regex pre-tokenization step.

Before BPE, tiktoken splits text using a regex pattern. For cl100k_base:
  (?i:'s|'t|'re|'ve|'m|'ll|'d)|[^\r\n\p{L}\p{N}]?\p{L}+|\p{N}{1,3}| ?[^\s\p{L}\p{N}]+[\r\n]*|\s*[\r\n]+|\s+(?\!\S)|\s+

This splits text into meaningful chunks before BPE is applied to each chunk independently.

Implement:
- pre_tokenize(text, pattern) -> [binary()]
- Compile regex at load time, not per-call
- Each encoding has its own pattern
- Handle Unicode correctly (Elixir's Regex module should handle this)
